data.title: As a commit workflow, severity-only pass/fail STATUS is derived without scores
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Update the review infrastructure (validate-review-output.sh, write-reviewer-findings.sh, record-review.sh) to accept a two-key schema (findings + summary) and derive STATUS purely from findings[].severity.
**Why**: This is the foundation change — all downstream agent and workflow updates depend on the infrastructure accepting the new format.
**Scope**:
- IN: validate-review-output.sh schema enforcement (remove scores requirement, update consistency checks), write-reviewer-findings.sh (remove scores normalization), record-review.sh (replace score-based STATUS with severity-based STATUS, remove score= from review-status file, remove HAS_CRITICAL/min_score computation)
- OUT: Reviewer agent definitions (Story 2), workflow/orchestrator references (Story 3), CLAUDE.md updates (Story 4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, validate-review-output.sh accepts reviewer-findings.json with exactly two top-level keys (findings, summary) and rejects files containing a scores key
  ← Satisfies: "The reviewer-findings.json schema requires exactly two top-level keys: findings and summary"
- When this story is complete, record-review.sh derives STATUS=failed when any finding has severity critical, important, or fragile, and STATUS=passed when all findings are minor or findings array is empty
  ← Satisfies: "record-review.sh derives STATUS exclusively from findings[].severity"
- When this story is complete, the review-status file contains no score= line
  ← Satisfies: "The review-status file no longer contains a score= line"
- When this story is complete, a minor finding whose description text contains the word "critical" or "important" does not cause STATUS=failed — only the structured severity field is checked
  ← Satisfies: "The gate never blocks based on text content of finding descriptions"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Review gate is safety-critical — test all 4 severity levels, edge cases (empty findings, all minor, mixed severities), and description-text-containment
- [Reliability] Plugin cache may retain stale agent definitions — ensure infrastructure gracefully handles both old (3-key) and new (2-key) schemas during transition

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
